title: Pouteria alnifolia
intro: Pouteria alnifolia, also known as the white walnut or canarium, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been linked to its potential in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria alnifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pouteria alnifolia is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or syrup, and is often combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Pouteria alnifolia is classified within the family Sapotaceae. This family consists of tropical evergreen trees and shrubs commonly known as sapotes. The genus Pouteria is a group of tropical trees native to Central and South America, Africa, and Australia.
